body.title: CLOSED: CC+/CC- tagging prompt in /fixit
body.result: added CC+/CC- tagging Step 0 to /fixit — asks classification before /decided fires, appends to slate ## CC score
body.description:
Rework events have no causal classification. The /fixit skill is the natural moment to tag each rework because that's exactly when rework is triggered. Adding a one-question prompt ('CC+ design changed, or CC- Claude gap?') and appending the event to the slate puts the signal at the right place in the workflow.

Proposed shape: at the start of /fixit (before ticket filing), prompt: 'Is this rework because the design changed (CC+) or because of a gap in my work (CC-)? One word: + or -'. Append the tagged event to '## CC score' section of today's slate: 'CC+/- HH:MM: <brief reason from context>'. If /fixit is called programmatically (no interactive context), skip the prompt and note as untagged.

**Affected files:** ~/.claude/skills/fixit (add tagging step at top)
**Design rules:** none
**Scope boundary:** one new step at /fixit entry. Not changing the rest of /fixit flow. Gate on T-cc-plus-minus-slate (slate section must exist for appending to work correctly).
**Test plan:** invoke /fixit for a rework scenario; verify CC+ or CC- prompt appears; verify entry appended to slate ## CC score section.
**Pass condition:** after /fixit run, today's slate contains a CC+/CC- entry under ## CC score with timestamp and reason.
